report_name: European Commission Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims 350GB Theft

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A notorious cyber extortion group that claimed responsibility for the breach of the European Commission's Europa.eu web portal, allegedly exfiltrating over 350GB of data from a compromised AWS account.
threat_actors.goals: Exfiltrate valuable data and extort the victim or sell the data on dark web forums.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The initial access vector was a compromised AWS account, which ShinyHunters used to gain entry into the European Commission's cloud environment.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exfiltrated over 350GB of sensitive data, including mail servers, databases, and documents, from the European Commission's AWS-hosted infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely aggregated the stolen data to a staging server or S3 bucket under their control before transferring it out of the victim's environment.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
attack_patterns.description: After gaining initial access, ShinyHunters performed reconnaissance within the AWS environment to identify valuable data stores like S3 buckets, RDS databases, and EC2 instance volumes.

identities.name: European Commission
identities.description: A governmental body of the European Union whose Europa.eu web portal was breached by ShinyHunters, resulting in the exfiltration of over 350GB of data from its AWS hosting environment.

identities.name: Amazon Web Services
identities.description: The cloud service provider hosting the European Commission's compromised Europa.eu web portal.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site where ShinyHunters released a 90GB sample of the data stolen from the European Commission to substantiate their claims and apply pressure.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Staging Server
infrastructure.description: A staging server or S3 bucket likely used by ShinyHunters to aggregate data before exfiltrating it from the European Commission's AWS environment.

indicators.name: Suspicious AWS API Activity for Discovery
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious AWS API Activity for Discovery
id: 2b8c1f9a-1e3d-4b5a-9c8d-7e6f5a4b3c2d
status: experimental
description: 'Monitors for suspicious AWS API calls like ListBuckets, GetObject, or CreateUser from unusual IP ranges or user agents, which could indicate reconnaissance activity as seen in the ShinyHunters breach of the European Commission.'
references:
  - https://therecord.media/european-commission-downplays-shinyhunters-cyberattack-impact
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1580
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventSource: 's3.amazonaws.com'
    eventName:
      - 'ListBuckets'
      - 'GetObject'
  selection_iam:
    eventSource: 'iam.amazonaws.com'
    eventName:
      - 'CreateUser'
      - 'ListUsers'
  filter_known_good:
    # Add known good IPs, UserAgents, or account ARNs here
    source_ip_address: '192.168.1.1'
  condition: (selection or selection_iam) and not filter_known_good
indicators.description: Detects suspicious AWS API calls such as ListBuckets, GetObject, or CreateUser from unusual sources, indicative of reconnaissance.

indicators.name: Anomalous AWS STS AssumeRole Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous AWS STS AssumeRole Activity
id: 8d9e0f1a-2b3c-4d5e-6f7a-8b9c0d1e2f3a
status: experimental
description: 'Detects anomalous AWS STS AssumeRole activity, especially if roles are assumed by external accounts or from unexpected geolocations. This can be an indicator of a compromised cloud account.'
references:
  - https://therecord.media/european-commission-downplays-shinyhunters-cyberattack-impact
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventSource: 'sts.amazonaws.com'
    eventName: 'AssumeRole'
  filter_external:
    userIdentity.type: 'AWSAccount'
    userIdentity.principalId|contains: # Add your own account ID here to filter for external accounts
      - 'AROA:'
  condition: selection and filter_external
indicators.description: Detects anomalous AWS `sts:AssumeRole` activity, particularly from external accounts or unexpected locations, which may indicate account compromise.

indicators.name: Anomalous High-Volume AWS Egress Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous High-Volume AWS Egress Traffic
id: 4a5b6c7d-8e9f-0a1b-2c3d-4e5f6a7b8c9d
status: experimental
description: 'Alerts on unusually large data transfers from S3 buckets or EC2 instances to external IP addresses, which could indicate data exfiltration.'
references:
  - https://therecord.media/european-commission-downplays-shinyhunters-cyberattack-impact
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1041
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: guardduty
detection:
  selection:
    type|startswith:
      - 'Exfiltration:S3'
      - 'Exfiltration:EC2'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects unusually large data transfers from S3 buckets or EC2 instances to external IP addresses, a potential sign of data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Anomalous AWS Console Login Location
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous AWS Console Login Location
id: 1e2f3a4b-5c6d-7e8f-9a0b-1c2d3e4f5a6b
status: experimental
description: 'Monitors for AWS console logins from geolocations inconsistent with known employee locations, which can indicate a compromised cloud account.'
references:
  - https://therecord.media/european-commission-downplays-shinyhunters-cyberattack-impact
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-01
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventName: 'ConsoleLogin'
    responseElements.ConsoleLogin: 'Success'
  filter_known_locations:
    # Add known good country codes here
    awsRegion: 'eu-central-1'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_locations
indicators.description: Detects AWS console logins from geolocations that are inconsistent with normal employee locations, suggesting credential compromise.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious AWS API Activity for Discovery
